data.title: BRICS Explores Digital Clearing System for Local-Currency Payments
data.description: BRICS nations are examining the development of a digital clearing mechanism aimed at facilitating local-currency payments. According to NS3.AI, experts suggest that this system could be operational by 2026. The proposed mechanism is intended to serve as a settlement system and unit of account, rather than a comprehensive shared currency.

data.title: Germany Warns of Potential Energy Shortage Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Germany's Federal Minister for Economic Affairs and Energy, Peter Altmaier, has warned of a potential energy shortage if the conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran continues. According to RTHK, Altmaier, speaking at an energy conference in Texas, emphasized that such a shortage could occur between late April and early May. He also criticized the phasing out of nuclear energy as a significant mistake and highlighted liquefied natural gas as a crucial solution to address the energy shortfall.

Additionally, German President Frank-Walter Steinmeier condemned the military actions by the United States and Israel against Iran, labeling them as clear violations of international law and a grave political error. During an event, Steinmeier remarked that since U.S. President Donald Trump began his second term, transatlantic relations have deteriorated, leading to a loss of political trust in the United States among allies and globally.

data.title: AI and Tokenization Lead Crypto Sector Growth Amid Geopolitical Challenges
data.description:
Grayscale Research has highlighted significant developments in the crypto sector despite macroeconomic volatility and geopolitical risks. Grayscale posted on X that tokenization saw a remarkable 245% year-on-year increase in tokenized assets. Additionally, stablecoin volumes have doubled compared to the previous year.

AI-linked tokens have also shown strong performance, driven by increasing demand for onchain infrastructure. These advancements suggest a more positive outlook for the crypto industry, even as broader market conditions remain challenging.

The report underscores the resilience of certain sectors within the crypto market, particularly those related to AI and tokenization, which have managed to thrive amid global uncertainties.

data.title: Nelson Peltz's Continued Interest in Janus Since 2020
data.description: Nelson Peltz has maintained a keen interest in Janus since 2020. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Peltz's ongoing focus on the company. His intentions appear to be aimed at completing the objectives he set out to achieve three years ago. Peltz's strategic moves have been closely watched by industry analysts, as he seeks to influence the direction of Janus. The developments surrounding his involvement continue to be a point of interest in the financial sector.

data.title: On Polymarket, the probability of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security ending its shutdown by the end of March is currently reported at 54%, down 26% in one hour.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the U.S. Senate has voted to approve Senator Marin as Secretary of Homeland Security. The probability of the "Department of Homeland Security shutdown ending March 31" event on Polymarket has dropped to 54%, a 26% decrease in one hour, with trading volume approaching $1 million. Meanwhile, the probability of the "Department of Homeland Security shutdown ending by the end of March" event saw a brief surge, currently at 24%, a 13% increase in one hour; the probability of the "Department of Homeland Security shutdown ending March 24-27" event is currently at 13%, a 3% increase in one hour. At her nomination hearing last week, Marin expressed support for the Trump administration's immigration policies. Marin also urged Democrats to quickly pass the budget bill for the Department of Homeland Security to "reopen" the department. During the hearing, Marin stated, "As Secretary of Homeland Security, I will protect everyone, and my goal for the next six months is to keep the Department of Homeland Security out of the headlines every day." Since last year, the Department of Homeland Security has frequently made headlines due to its efforts to combat illegal immigration and other issues. Democrats are demanding reforms to the Department of Homeland Security and federal law enforcement, threatening not to support a budget for the department if it fails to do so. The Department of Homeland Security has been shut down since February 14 due to running out of funds. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing them in.

data.title: SlowMist: Attackers have stolen approximately 300GB of data due to the LiteLLM vulnerability.
data.description: 23pds (Shan Ge), Chief Information Security Officer of SlowMist, posted on the X platform that reports indicate attackers exploiting the LiteLLM vulnerability have stolen approximately 300GB of data and about 500,000 credentials. He advised all cryptocurrency developers to immediately review and rotate relevant keys and credentials as soon as possible, and to check logs, access records, and sensitive data exposure to avoid losses similar to the Trust Wallet incident. Previously, it was reported that LiteLLM, with 97 million monthly downloads, was attacked by the supply chain, allowing for the theft of SSH keys and all sensitive credentials with a simple installation.

data.title: A whale withdrew 33,998 ETH from Kraken, worth approximately $72.9 million.
data.description: According to The Data Nerd, a whale (0xD77...A7Bb) withdrew 33,998 ETH from Kraken 5 hours ago, worth approximately $72.9 million.

data.title: Significant Positions in BTC and Oil on Hyperliquid
data.description:
According to Odaily, monitoring data from Ai Yi indicates that the address 0x94d...33814 has opened significant positions on Hyperliquid from last night until early morning. The address is shorting Bitcoin (BTC) while simultaneously going long on crude oil, with a total position value reaching $89.79 million, making it the top BTC position on Hyperliquid.

The BTC short position involves 1,000 BTC with a value of $70.65 million, entered at a price of $69,614. The long position in Brent crude oil involves 202,000 units valued at $19.16 million, with an entry price of $98.32. Both positions are currently experiencing unrealized losses, with a total loss exceeding $1.7 million.

data.title: South Korea Considers Repealing Virtual Asset Tax
data.description: South Korea's People Power Party is set to conduct a public hearing to discuss the potential repeal of the country's virtual asset tax. According to NS3.AI, this move comes amid growing debates on the impact of such taxes on the digital asset market and its participants. The hearing aims to gather insights and opinions from various stakeholders to assess the implications of the tax policy on the burgeoning cryptocurrency sector in South Korea.

data.title: ITcen Global Joins Hana Financial Group's Stablecoin Consortium
data.description: ITcen Global has become a member of Hana Financial Group's consortium for distributing won-backed stablecoins, as reported by SBS Biz. According to NS3.AI, this development is perceived as a potential precursor to collaboration on a gold-linked stablecoin.

data.title: The crypto market generally rose, with the AI ​​sector surging over 10%, while only the RWA sector saw a slight decline.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the crypto market generally maintained an upward trend. The AI ​​sector performed exceptionally well, rising 10.58% in the last 24 hours. Among them, Siren (SIREN), which experienced a significant drop yesterday, rebounded sharply, rising 48.37%. Bittensor (TAO) and Venice Token (VVV) rose 14.61% and 18.15% respectively. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 0.48%, remaining above $70,000, while Ethereum (ETH) rose 1.39%, fluctuating narrowly around the $2,100 mark. In other sectors, the Meme sector rose 3.58% in the last 24 hours, with Fartcoin (FARTCOIN) up 7.89%; the DeFi sector rose 3.02%, with Hyperliquid (HYPE) up 8.24%; the Layer 2 sector rose 1.75%, with Mantle (MNT) up 1.85%; the Layer 1 sector rose 1.23%, with Zcash (ZEC) up 7.07%; the CeFi sector rose 0.92%, with FTX (FTT) up 5.30%; and the PayFi sector rose 0.89%, with Stellar (XLM) up 9.20%. Additionally, only the RWA sector saw a slight decline of 0.15%, with Sky (SKY) falling 2.18% within the sector, but Ondo Finance (ONDO) rising slightly by 1.31%. The crypto sector indices, which reflect the historical performance of the sector, show that the ssiAI, ssiDeFi, and ssiMeme indices rose by 8.58%, 4.74%, and 2.21%, respectively.

data.title: Hyperliquid Platform Whale Holdings Reach $3.792 Billion
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, data from Coinglass reveals that the Hyperliquid platform's whale currently holds positions valued at $3.792 billion. The long positions amount to $1.882 billion, representing 49.64% of the total, while short positions are at $1.91 billion, accounting for 50.36%. The long positions have incurred a loss of $22.7988 million, whereas the short positions have gained $53.0511 million.

Notably, a whale address, 0xa5b0..41, has taken a 15x leveraged long position on ETH at a price of $2,148.7, with an unrealized profit and loss of $496,600.

data.title: Meta Reintroduces Stock Options for Executives Amid AI Competition
data.description: Meta is reintroducing stock options for its top executives for the first time since its initial public offering in 2012. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of the company's strategy to retain and reward its leadership team. This decision comes as Meta continues to invest heavily in artificial intelligence to maintain its competitive edge in the rapidly evolving tech landscape. The company aims to ensure that its executives are aligned with its long-term goals as it navigates the challenges and opportunities presented by the AI sector.

data.title: Thailand's Finance Minister: Government to Allow Energy Prices to Fluctuate with Market Mechanisms
data.description: Thailand's Finance Minister has announced that the government will permit energy prices to fluctuate freely according to market mechanisms. According to Jin10, this decision reflects a shift towards a more market-driven approach in managing energy costs. The move is expected to impact various sectors, potentially leading to adjustments in consumer prices and business operations. The Finance Minister emphasized the importance of aligning energy pricing with global market trends to ensure economic stability and competitiveness. This policy change comes amid broader efforts to enhance Thailand's economic resilience and adaptability in the face of fluctuating global energy markets.

data.title: Thailand's Finance Minister Plans Measures to Mitigate Rising Fuel Prices
data.description: Thailand's Finance Minister has announced plans to introduce support measures aimed at alleviating the impact of rising fuel prices. According to Jin10, the government is considering various strategies to address the economic challenges posed by increasing fuel costs. These measures are expected to provide relief to consumers and businesses affected by the price hikes. The move comes as part of a broader effort to stabilize the economy and ensure sustainable growth amid global economic uncertainties. Further details on the specific measures and their implementation timeline are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: Oil Prices Drop Over 5% as Gold Rises Amid U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Talks
data.description:
International oil prices have fallen by more than 5% to over 6%, while gold prices have increased by over 2%. According to RTHK, reports indicate that the United States is seeking a one-month ceasefire with Iran. This development has fueled market hopes for the resumption of oil exports from the Persian Gulf.

During Asian trading hours, both Brent crude and U.S. crude futures experienced declines. Brent crude was recently priced at $97.87 per barrel, marking a 6.3% decrease. Meanwhile, New York crude was trading at $87.3 per barrel, reflecting a drop of approximately 5.5%.

In contrast, spot gold prices have surged, nearing $4,600 per ounce. Earlier, gold was priced at $4,585.1 per ounce, representing a 2.5% increase.

data.title: Analysts predict oil prices will remain high until the conflict with Iran ends.
data.description: Oil prices fell sharply as the US pushed for an end to the war with Iran. Ajay Parmar, head of oil markets at commodities research firm ICIS, said, "Oil prices will continue to fluctuate because the market is influenced by both fundamental factors and rhetoric. The fundamentals haven't changed—there are still nearly 10 million barrels per day of oil supply disruptions in the Middle East, and we expect oil prices to remain high until this war ends." (Jinshi)

data.title: Disney CEO Faces Challenges with Billion-Dollar Tech Investments
data.description: Disney CEO Josh D’Amaro, who recently assumed his position, is already encountering significant challenges as two major technology investments face setbacks. Bloomberg posted on X that one of these investments has completely unraveled, marking a difficult start for D’Amaro in his new role. The situation underscores the complexities and risks associated with large-scale technological ventures in the entertainment industry. As Disney navigates these challenges, the company's strategic direction under D’Amaro's leadership will be closely watched by industry analysts and investors.

data.title: Bitcoin Wallet Linked to Clifton Collins Activated After Decade of Inactivity
data.description: A Bitcoin wallet associated with Clifton Collins has reportedly been activated after approximately 10 years of dormancy, with 500 BTC transferred to a different address. According to NS3.AI, Collins had amassed 6,000 BTC across 12 addresses. The recent transaction indicates that not all of these holdings were lost.

data.title: House Republicans Reject Senate's Legislative Proposal
data.description: House Republicans have declined a legislative proposal from the Senate. According to Odaily, the proposal suggested incorporating the House's bipartisan community bank deregulation bill into pending cryptocurrency market structure legislation in exchange for the House's acceptance of the Senate-passed affordable housing bill. Rep. Bill Huizenga, Vice Chair of the House Financial Services Committee, expressed skepticism about the proposal. Last week, Senate Banking Committee Republicans discussed the proposal in a closed-door meeting, with Senator Katie Britt assisting in presenting it to other Republican senators. The House had included community bank deregulation provisions in a housing bill passed in February, but the Senate's housing bill passed this month did not include these provisions. House Republicans stated that several issues remain in the Senate's housing bill, and simply adding the banking bill to cryptocurrency legislation is insufficient for them to accept the Senate's version of the housing bill. Rep. Andy Barr, a senior member of the House Financial Services Committee, supports including banking regulation provisions in cryptocurrency legislation but also demands that the Senate incorporate parts of the House's housing proposals.
